column3: A short voice sample is used to synthesize a relative’s voice. The caller claims a crash, arrest, kidnapping, or medical emergency and demands irreversible payment via wire, cryptocurrency, gift cards, or courier.
column5: FTC (2023-03-20): Scammers use AI to enhance their family emergency schemes; FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud; FBI (2025-12-05): Criminals Using Altered Proof-of-Life Media to Extort Victims in Virtual Kidnapping for Ransom Scams
column7: 2025: IC3 2025: victims reported over $5 million in losses to AI-enabled distress scams — $5,000,000 (at-least)

column2: Deepfake executive impersonation and business email compromise
column3: Voice-cloned or deepfake video avatars of executives, CFOs, vendors, or lawyers are used to direct employees to transfer funds, change invoices, or disclose credentials. AI is combined with compromised email, messaging apps, and fake virtual meetings.
column5: FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud; FinCEN (2024-11-13): FinCEN Alert on Fraud Schemes Involving Deepfake Media Targeting Financial Institutions
column7: 2025: IC3 2025: businesses reported over $30 million in losses to BEC scams involving AI — $30,000,000 (at-least)

column2: AI-enhanced romance and pig-butchering investment scams
column3: AI-generated personas, images, scripts, translations, and chatbots scale romance and trust-based investment schemes and crypto-investment fraud. Victims are moved to private messaging apps and shown fake trading profits on sham platforms.
column4: pig butchering; romance scam; sha zhu pan
column5: FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud; FBI (2025-07-03): Fraudsters Target US Stock Investors through Investment Clubs Accessed on Social Media and Messaging Applications; FinCEN (2023-09-08): FinCEN Alert on Prevalent Virtual Currency Investment Scam Commonly Known as ‘Pig Butchering’

column3: AI-generated video, images, or voice of celebrities, CEOs, or public figures is used in social ads, investment-club funnels, or private chats to make fraudulent investment opportunities appear endorsed.
column5: FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
column7: 2025: IC3 2025: investment complaints with reported AI nexus surpassed $632 million — $632,000,000 (at-least)

column2: Fake AI tools and deceptive AI services
column3: Operators sell AI-branded products that do not work as promised or generate deceptive content. Categories include an alleged “AI lawyer,” AI-branded money-making storefronts, AI fake-review generators, and AI telemarketing tools. Criminals also embed AI chatbots in fraudulent websites to automate victim engagement.
column5: FTC (2024-09-25): FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes; FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
column7: 2024-09-25: FTC Operation AI Comply: actions against AI fake-review tool, alleged AI lawyer, and AI-branded money-making storefront schemes; 2026-03-24: FTC Air AI Technologies final order entered (case 2:25-cv-03068-SMB)
column9: FTC v. DoNotPay (alleged AI lawyer); FTC v. Rytr (AI fake reviews); FTC v. Air AI (AI telemarketing); FTC v. Ascend Ecom / Empire Holdings (AI storefronts); FTC v. FBA Machine / Passive Scaling (AI storefronts)

column2: AI-generated phishing and spear phishing at scale
column3: Generative AI lowers the cost of producing convincing targeted lures, social profiles, translations, spear-phishing content, and fake support flows. Attackers steal credentials, MFA codes, contact lists, or financial account access.
column5: FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud; FBI/CISA (2026-03-20): Russian Intelligence Services Target Commercial Messaging Application Accounts; FBI (2024-04-11): Cyber Criminals Target Victims Using Social Engineering Techniques
column7: 2025: IC3 2025: phishing/spoofing generated 191,561 complaints and $215,843,126 in losses (not all AI-enabled) — $215,843,126 (complaint-reported-aggregate)

column2: AI-generated sextortion, nonconsensual intimate deepfakes, and AI CSAM
column3: Criminals generate or alter sexual imagery using AI, then extort victims or distribute the material. The FBI states that AI-generated CSAM is illegal under federal law. The Take It Down Act created new criminal penalties for publishing nonconsensual intimate digital forgeries.
column5: FBI (2024-03-29): Child Sexual Abuse Material Created by Generative AI and Similar Online Tools is Illegal; FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
column7: 2025: IC3 2025: more than 75,000 sextortion submissions; more than 5,700 involving minors referred to NCMEC; 2026-04-07: DOJ S.D. Ohio: James Strahler II pleaded guilty to cyberstalking, producing obscene visual representations of child sexual abuse, and publication of digital forgeries using AI; 2026-05-20: DOJ E.D.N.Y.: Shannon and Hernandez arrested for publishing AI deepfake pornography in violation of the Take It Down Act

column2: AI voice phishing and IT-helpdesk social engineering
column3: Attackers impersonate employees or support staff, call help desks, and request MFA resets, SIM swaps, call forwarding, or credential resets. FBI IC3 PSA I-120324 notes AI voice can make vishing more credible. CISA/FBI advisory AA23-320A documents related Scattered Spider social-engineering and intrusion tactics but does not specifically address AI voice.
column4: Scattered Spider; vishing; helpdesk social engineering
column5: FBI (2024-04-11): Cyber Criminals Target Victims Using Social Engineering Techniques; CISA/FBI (2023-11-16): Scattered Spider; FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

column2: AI-generated fake reviews and deceptive listings
column3: AI writing tools generate fabricated testimonials and reviews at high volume. AI images create fake photos for products, rentals, or listings. The FTC finalized a rule banning fake reviews and testimonials, including those generated by AI.
column5: FTC (2024-09-25): FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes; FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud
column9: FTC v. Rytr (AI fake-review enforcement); FTC v. SiteJabber (review manipulation)

column2: Impersonation of government officials via AI voice and text
column3: Malicious actors send texts and AI-generated voice messages purporting to be senior U.S. officials. They seek rapport, migration to encrypted messaging apps, credential theft, contact-list compromise, or funds.
column5: FBI (2025-05-15): Senior US Officials Impersonated in Malicious Messaging Campaign; FBI (2025-12-19): Senior U.S. Officials Continue to be Impersonated in Malicious Messaging Campaign
column7: 2025: FTC 2025 data: government impersonators caused about $920 million in losses (not all AI-enabled) — $920,000,000 (complaint-reported-aggregate)
column9: FCC v. Lingo Telecom (AI robocall settlement); FCC v. Steve Kramer (deepfake Biden robocalls)

column2: Deepfake synthetic identity and KYC fraud
column3: Deepfake images, fake identity documents, synthetic media, and spoofed biometrics are used to bypass financial-institution identity verification and open or access accounts. FinCEN reports increased suspicious activity reporting describing suspected deepfake media.
column5: FinCEN (2024-11-13): FinCEN Alert on Fraud Schemes Involving Deepfake Media Targeting Financial Institutions; FBI (2024-12-03): Criminals Use Generative Artificial Intelligence to Facilitate Financial Fraud

column2: Election deepfake robocalls and public-figure voice cloning
column3: AI voice cloning creates a public figure’s voice and robocall networks distribute the message with spoofed caller ID to mislead voters or impersonate public officials.
column5: FCC (2024-05-24): FCC NAL FCC 24-59: Steve Kramer deepfake Biden robocalls; FCC (2024-02-06): FCC cease-and-desist to Lingo Telecom
